Coverage

Coverage built around practical rollout and operational control

SLYDE is being launched with a Jamaica-first network strategy that prioritizes dense demand zones, controlled service quality, and future regional repeatability.

Discuss Business CoverageView Slyder Opportunities

Coverage

Jamaica-first operations with a Caribbean scale path

SLYDE is launching with a practical rollout model: high-density urban coverage first, staged zone expansion second, and repeatable operating standards for future markets.

Launching soon

Kingston - 92% ready

One of the strongest early launch areas, with Slyder onboarding moving toward final readiness.

Building network

Mandeville - we need more Slyders

This area is in network-building phase, and early applicants help move it closer to launch.

Final onboarding phase

Montego Bay - near ready

Courier strength is growing and the area is moving closer to launch readiness.

Parish hotspot watch

Courier interest is forming across towns in all 14 parishes

SLYDE is not presenting every parish as live. This board shows where courier attention, early applications, merchant visibility, and launch-watch signals are being built so Jamaica-wide readiness can grow with more discipline.

14 parishes tracked

Island-wide hotspot monitoring

Growth posture

Interest, applications, launch watch

Kingston

Downtown Kingston • Half-Way Tree • Cross Roads

Launch watch

Hotspot towns under active visibility

Courier interest is already concentrated here, with applications and merchant conversations building around dense delivery corridors.

Slyder momentum forming

St Andrew

Constant Spring • Papine • Portmore Edge Corridors

Early applications

Hotspot towns under active visibility

High commuter and retail movement makes this a natural operating extension as SLYDE shapes launch-ready courier pockets.

Slyder momentum forming

St Thomas

Morant Bay • Yallahs • Seaforth

Interest building

Hotspot towns under active visibility

SLYDE is monitoring early courier interest here so eastern routes can be developed with stronger local readiness over time.

Slyder momentum forming

Portland

Port Antonio • Buff Bay • Hope Bay

Network scouting

Hotspot towns under active visibility

This parish is being watched for strategic hotspot growth, especially around tourism, town movement, and local merchant demand.

Slyder momentum forming

St Mary

Port Maria • Annotto Bay • Oracabessa

Interest building

Hotspot towns under active visibility

Courier awareness is being cultivated in St Mary so launch posture can strengthen along active town and highway links.

Slyder momentum forming

St Ann

Ocho Rios • St Ann's Bay • Runaway Bay

Early applications

Hotspot towns under active visibility

Tourism and retail density make St Ann a visible hotspot for early Slyder applications and merchant-side interest.

Slyder momentum forming

Trelawny

Falmouth • Wakefield • Duncans

Interest building

Hotspot towns under active visibility

SLYDE is framing Trelawny as an emerging logistics corridor as more courier participation starts to gather around growth towns.

Slyder momentum forming

St James

Montego Bay • Anchovy • Ironshore

Launch watch

Hotspot towns under active visibility

Montego Bay remains one of the most visible rollout hotspots, with onboarding activity reinforcing a stronger launch case.

Slyder momentum forming

Hanover

Lucea • Sandy Bay • Hopewell

Network scouting

Hotspot towns under active visibility

Hanover is being positioned as a west-coast growth parish where early courier interest can mature into stronger readiness later.

Slyder momentum forming

Westmoreland

Savanna-la-Mar • Negril • Whithorn

Early applications

Hotspot towns under active visibility

Courier and tourism-linked movement makes Westmoreland a meaningful parish for early application momentum and hotspot watching.

Slyder momentum forming

St Elizabeth

Santa Cruz • Black River • Southfield

Interest building

Hotspot towns under active visibility

SLYDE is shaping local visibility here so courier formation can expand outward from known town and market routes.

Slyder momentum forming

Manchester

Mandeville • Christiana • Porus

Launch watch

Hotspot towns under active visibility

Manchester remains a clear focus area, with Mandeville continuing to signal strong need and visible network-building opportunity.

Slyder momentum forming

Clarendon

May Pen • Chapelton • Lionel Town

Interest building

Hotspot towns under active visibility

Clarendon is being tracked as a central parish where courier interest and merchant movement can support future readiness growth.

Slyder momentum forming

St Catherine

Spanish Town • Old Harbour • Linstead

Early applications

Hotspot towns under active visibility

St Catherine is positioned as a major network feeder zone, with strong population density and multiple town-level growth points.

Slyder momentum forming

Current service areas

The launch focus is on the Kingston metropolitan area and adjacent demand corridors.

Rollout zones

Additional urban zones are evaluated based on merchant demand, courier density, and operational readiness.

Business availability

Coverage for businesses can differ by use case, service level, and operational partnership model.

Jamaica-First Logistics Infrastructure

SLYDE connects courier capacity, merchant fulfillment, and partner-ready logistics workflows.

Built for local operational credibility now, with standards that can support wider Caribbean scale later.
SLYDELogistics

SLYDE is a modern logistics network for Jamaica first, designed to support courier operations, merchant fulfillment, and future Caribbean partner integrations.
